All-c..Generalities: The patient and all the phases of his 'cold,' his coryza, his laryngitis, his cough, all his complaints, are aggravated by warmth, are worse in a warm room. Most of the symptoms are worse in the evening, the symptoms of coryza, the 'cold,' and the general symptoms.
All-c..Head: Dull frontal headaches, occipital headaches; headaches very severe and the eyes cannot stand the light; tearing, bursting, throbbing in the head.
All-c..Rectum: Allium cepa also cures a ragged, sensitive condition of the anus, with bleeding, in infants.
All-c..Clinical: It will wipe out an attack of hay fever in a few days, when the symptoms agree. Allium cepa is a wonderful remedy in whooping cough. Cepa has a record for croupy cough. Another affection over which this remedy has marvellous power is traumatic neuritis, often met with in a stump after amputation.
All-c..Abdomen: Cutting, rending, tearing pains, drawing the poor little thing almost double. It screams with the violent cutting in the lower abdomen.
All-c..Head, pain: Pains through the jaws, in the face; and these pains extend into the head. Dull frontal headaches, occipital headaches; headaches very severe and the eyes cannot stand the light; tearing, bursting, throbbing in the head.
All-c..Eye: The discharge from the eye is bland. Although there is burning in the eyes the tears do not excoriate as they flow down over the cheek.
All-c..Eye, lachrymation: Profuse, bland lachrymation. Lachrymation in the evening in a warm room.
All-c..Eye, pain: Although there is burning in the eyes the tears do not excoriate as they flow down over the cheek.
All-c..Larynx and trachea: Violent inflammation of the larynx coming on very rapidly, with that cough I spoke of, and the tearing in the larynx.
All-c..Larynx and trachea, voice, hoarseness: Tickling in the larynx with hoarseness.
All-c..Larynx and trachea, voice: Acute complaints of the voice; catarrhal hoarseness; copious expectoration of mucus from the larynx.
All-c..Abdomen, colic: Colicky pains beginning in the hepatic region and spreading over the whole abdomen, worse around the navel; worse when sitting.
All-c..Clinical, colic: We all know what a flatulent vegetable the onion is. It is a wonderful medicine for babies with colic.
All-c..Cough: Hoarse, harsh, ringing, spasmodic cough, excited by constant tickling in the larynx; cough produces a raw, splitting pain in the larynx, so acute and so severe as to compel the patient to crouch from suffering and to make every effort to suppress the cough.
All-c..Cough, tickling: Cough, excited by tickling in the larynx and when lying down at night in a warm room.
All-c..Generalities, mucous secretions increase: Rawness in the nose, copious flow of water from the eyes; copious watery discharge from the nose. Copious expectoration of mucus from the larynx.
All-c..Nose: A watery discharge drips from the nose constantly, burns like fire, and excoriates the upper lip and the wings of the nose until there are rawness and redness. Notice that the fluid from the nose is excoriating and the fluid from the eyes bland.
All-c..Clinical, inflammation: Violent inflammation of the larynx coming on very rapidly.
All-c..Larynx and trachea, tickling: The tickling in the larynx, which is sometimes aggravated by drawing in cold air. A warm draft will so increase the tickling that it is sure to set the patient coughing.
All-c..Larynx and trachea, larynx in general: Rawness in the larynx and throat, extending down into the chest. Some will describe it as a sensation as if something were being torn loose. Those who describe more accurately will say that it feels as if a hook were dragging up through the larynx with every cough.
Cough and difficult breathing from inspiring cold-air, yet a warm draft will so increase the tickling that it is sure to set the patient coughing. So the cough is aggravated both by cold air and a warm room.
All-c..Cough, hacking: Severe, laryngeal cough, which compels the patient to grasp the larynx; feels as if cough would tear it.
All-c..Cough, hacking, tickling in larynx, from: Hoarse, harsh, ringing, spasmodic cough, excited by constant tickling in the larynx; cough produces a raw, splitting pain in the larynx.
All-c..Cough, hoarse: Hoarse, harsh, ringing, spasmodic cough, excited by constant tickling in the larynx.
